title: Email Not Working
cardText: Diagnose email problems such as not sending, not receiving, login failures, sync issues, and attachment problems.
Check internet access, webmail, account credentials, storage limits, mail client settings, and server status.
Separate device/app issues from account, provider, and mail server faults.
text: This guide is for email accounts that are not sending, receiving, syncing, or logging in correctly.
Start by identifying whether the issue affects webmail, one device, or all devices.
Check internet access, account login, storage, spam folders, sync status, and mail client configuration.
Escalate provider or account problems when local checks do not resolve the issue.

identifyEmailProblem.title: Identify the email problem
body: Determine what exactly is not working.
Common symptoms include cannot send, cannot receive, login failed, password prompt, emails missing, attachments failing, or email not syncing.
The exact symptom determines the correct troubleshooting path.

checkInternetAccess.title: Check internet access
body: Open a browser and test several websites.
Email apps need a working internet connection to send, receive, and sync messages.
If websites do not load, troubleshoot the internet connection first.

useNoInternetGuide.title: Use the no-internet guide
body: The device does not have working internet access.
Use the No Internet Connection guide before troubleshooting email.
Email cannot work reliably until the network connection is restored.

testWebmail.title: Test webmail access
body: Open the email provider's webmail page in a browser.
Sign in and check whether email works there.
If webmail works, the account is probably fine and the issue is likely with the email app or device.

checkProviderStatus.title: Check provider status
body: Check whether the email provider has a service outage.
Provider outages can stop sending, receiving, login, or webmail access.
If the provider is down, local troubleshooting will not fix the issue.

waitForProvider.title: Wait for provider restoration
body: The email provider appears to have an outage.
Wait for the provider to restore service.
Retest webmail and the email app after the outage is resolved.

checkAccountCredentials.title: Check account credentials
body: Confirm the email address and password are correct.
If multi-factor authentication is enabled, confirm the sign-in approval is completed.
Some email apps require an app password instead of the normal account password.

resetEmailPassword.title: Reset or recover email account
body: The account cannot be accessed with the current credentials.
Use the provider's account recovery or password reset process.
After access is restored, update the password in all email apps and devices.

checkMailboxStorage.title: Check mailbox storage
body: Check whether the mailbox is full or close to its storage limit.
A full mailbox may prevent new mail from arriving or stop outgoing messages from being saved.
Delete unnecessary mail or upgrade storage if required.

freeMailboxStorage.title: Free mailbox storage
body: Delete or archive old email, empty deleted items, and remove large attachments if safe.
After freeing space, wait a few minutes and test sending and receiving again.
Mail delivery may take time to resume after storage is cleared.

checkSpamAndRules.title: Check spam, junk, and mail rules
body: Check spam, junk, deleted items, archive, and other folders.
Review mail rules or filters that may move messages automatically.
Missing emails are often being delivered but moved somewhere unexpected.

mailRuleIssue.title: Mail rule or folder issue found
body: The missing email was found in another folder or moved by a rule.
Adjust spam settings, safe sender lists, or mailbox rules as needed.
Ask the sender to resend if the message was deleted or quarantined.

checkSendOrReceive.title: Determine whether sending or receiving fails
body: Test sending an email to yourself.
Then ask whether incoming mail, outgoing mail, or both are failing.
Sending and receiving use different server paths, so the distinction matters.

checkIncomingMail.title: Check incoming mail
body: Send a test email from another account to the affected address.
Check whether it appears in webmail.
If it reaches webmail but not the app, the issue is local sync.

incomingServerIssue.title: Incoming mail delivery issue likely
body: Incoming messages are not reaching webmail.
Check sender bounce-back messages, mailbox storage, domain records, provider filtering, or account restrictions.
Escalate to the email provider or domain administrator if needed.

checkOutgoingMail.title: Check outgoing mail
body: Send a plain test email with no attachment.
Large attachments, blocked recipients, bad addresses, or server authentication problems can stop sending.
Check for bounce-back or error messages.

attachmentOrRecipientIssue.title: Attachment or recipient issue likely
body: A plain email sends successfully, so the account can send mail.
The original message may have an attachment that is too large, a blocked file type, or an invalid recipient.
Compress the file, use cloud sharing, or correct the recipient address.

checkOutbox.title: Check outbox
body: Open the outbox or drafts folder.
A stuck message can block later messages from sending.
Delete or move stuck messages, then test with a small plain email.

clearOutbox.title: Clear stuck outbox message
body: Delete or move the stuck message from the outbox.
Restart the email app and send a small test email.
Recreate the original message only after basic sending works.

checkSmtpSettings.title: Check outgoing server settings
body: Check the outgoing SMTP server, port, encryption, and authentication settings.
Incorrect SMTP settings commonly prevent sending while receiving still works.
Use the provider's current recommended settings.

fixSmtpSettings.title: Correct outgoing mail settings
body: Update the SMTP server settings using the provider's recommended values.
Confirm authentication is enabled if required.
Restart the email app and test sending again.

checkLoginProblem.title: Check login or sync symptom
body: Determine whether the issue is a password prompt, login failure, account disconnected message, or mail not syncing.
Login failures usually point to credentials, authentication, account lockout, or provider issues.
Sync failures may be local app, cache, profile, or connection related.

checkMailAppIssue.title: Check whether only the mail app is affected
body: If webmail works but the email app does not, the account itself is likely working.
The issue is probably the mail app, saved password, sync cache, profile, or account configuration.
This is common after password changes, updates, or account security changes.

restartMailApp.title: Restart the mail app and device
body: Close and reopen the email app.
Restart the device if the app still does not sync.
Temporary sync and authentication problems can sometimes clear after a restart.

checkSavedPassword.title: Check saved password
body: If the password was recently changed, update the saved password in the email app.
Remove old saved credentials if the app keeps using the wrong password.
For business or hosted accounts, sign-in may also require MFA or an app password.

removeAndReaddAccount.title: Remove and re-add the email account
body: Remove the email account from the app if webmail confirms the account is working.
Add the account again using automatic setup if available.
This refreshes server settings, authentication, and sync configuration.

checkSecuritySoftware.title: Check antivirus, firewall, or VPN interference
body: Security software, firewall rules, or VPN connections can block mail traffic.
Temporarily disconnect VPN or check security software mail protection settings if safe.
Do not disable security protection permanently without understanding the cause.

securitySoftwareIssue.title: Security software or VPN issue found
body: Mail works after changing VPN, firewall, or security software settings.
Update the security software configuration rather than leaving protection disabled.
Document what was changed in case the issue returns.

finalEmailEscalation.title: Further email diagnostics required
body: Basic account, webmail, storage, app, server, and security checks did not resolve the issue.
Next steps may include checking provider logs, domain DNS records, mailbox licensing, account restrictions, or rebuilding the mail profile.
Escalate to the email provider, domain administrator, or IT support.
